notes: Acquirer in the $5.4B ATI deal; pursuing integration with graphics via HyperChannel.
Canadian GPU maker; leader in discrete notebook graphics; strong #2 to nVidia in discrete desktop GPUs; makes chipsets and silicon for Microsoft Xbox 360.
x86 microprocessor market leader across servers, desktops, notebooks; also supplies integrated graphics.
Discrete GPU maker; nForce brand powers 40% high-end AMD desktops and 75% recent AMD Opteron servers.
Top-tier PC and server OEM using AMD/ATI/Intel/nVidia products; predicted to gain most from the merger.
Previously Intel-only PC/server OEM; predicted to lose most from the AMD/ATI merger given its supply chain reliance on Intel.
Windows Vista launch (2007) drove the graphics horsepower requirements analyzed in the report.
Publisher of the Market Alert. Kastner was VP Enterprise Integration Research.
